title: Client Scope Document
promptText:
You are a project management consultant who specializes in creating clear, comprehensive scope documents that prevent scope creep and protect both freelancers and clients. Create a detailed project scope document for the following engagement:

Project Name: [PROJECT_NAME]
Client Name: [CLIENT_NAME]
Service Type: [SERVICE_TYPE]
Project Overview: [PROJECT_OVERVIEW]
Key Deliverables: [DELIVERABLES]
Timeline: [TIMELINE]
Budget: [BUDGET]

The scope document should include the following sections:

1. Project Overview: Clear description of the project goals, business context, and desired outcomes.

2. Scope of Work: Detailed breakdown of exactly what is included. List each deliverable with a specific description and acceptance criteria.

3. Out of Scope: Explicitly list 5-8 items that are NOT included to prevent scope creep. Be specific to this project type.

4. Assumptions and Dependencies: What conditions must be true for the project to succeed on time and on budget? What does the client need to provide and by when?

5. Timeline and Milestones: Phase-by-phase schedule with specific deliverables, review periods, and approval gates.

6. Revision Policy: Number of revision rounds included, what constitutes a revision vs. a new request, and pricing for additional rounds.

7. Change Order Process: How scope changes are requested, evaluated, priced, and approved.

8. Payment Schedule: Milestone-based payment terms tied to deliverable approval.

9. Communication Protocol: Meeting cadence, response time expectations, primary contact, and tools used.

10. Acceptance Criteria: How the client formally approves each deliverable and the final project.

Format this as a professional document ready for client review and signature.
description: This prompt creates a comprehensive project scope document that protects freelancers from scope creep while setting clear expectations with clients. Scope creep is the number one profitability killer for freelancers, and this document prevents it by explicitly defining what is included, what is not, how changes are handled, and how work is approved. The output covers every section that professional project managers include in their scope documents — from revision policies and change order processes to communication protocols and acceptance criteria. It is essential for freelancers starting new client engagements, agencies onboarding projects, and consultants who want to professionalize their client relationships and avoid the costly misunderstandings that come from informal agreements.

variables.name: PROJECT_OVERVIEW
variables.example: Complete brand identity redesign including logo, color palette, typography, and brand guidelines for an organic food company repositioning from local to national market

variables.name: DELIVERABLES
variables.example: Logo (primary + secondary + icon), color palette, typography system, brand guidelines PDF, business card design, letterhead, social media templates (5)

tips: Be thorough with the Out of Scope section — this is where most freelancer-client disputes originate
Share the document before starting work and get written approval, even a simple email confirmation
Use the Change Order Process section any time a client asks for something outside the original scope
exampleOutput:
PROJECT SCOPE DOCUMENT
Project: Brand Identity Redesign — GreenLeaf Organics
Date: April 2, 2026
Prepared by: [Your Name]

1. PROJECT OVERVIEW
GreenLeaf Organics is repositioning from a local organic food brand to a national presence. This project will create a complete brand identity system that communicates premium quality, sustainability, and accessibility at a national scale.

2. SCOPE OF WORK
Deliverable 1: Primary Logo Design
  Description: Full-color logo with horizontal and stacked variations
  Acceptance Criteria: Scalable from favicon to billboard, works on light and dark backgrounds, provided in AI, EPS, PNG, and SVG formats
  Included: 3 initial concepts, 2 rounds of revisions on selected direction

3. OUT OF SCOPE
The following items are NOT included in this engagement:
- Website design or development
- Photography or illustration beyond logo marks
- Copywriting or tagline development
- Print production or print management
- Packaging design...
metaTitle: Client Scope Document Prompt for Claude
metaDescription: Create professional project scope documents that prevent scope creep with this free Claude prompt for freelancers and agencies.
